task_completion.description: Dashboard is created and named 'Storage Thrashing Analysis'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite dashboard interface is visible with the title 'Storage Thrashing Analysis' displayed at the top.

task_completion.description: Graph 1 is created with the correct title and targets
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Compute vs Storage Throughput' is visible. Its legend or target configuration shows 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization' and 'Disk Write (MB)'.

task_completion.description: Graph 2 is created with the correct title and divideSeries formula
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Thrashing Index' is visible. Its legend shows 'CPU per MB Written', indicating the divideSeries and scale functions were applied.

task_completion.description: Graph 3 is created with the correct title and moving median filter
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Smoothed Thrashing Trend' is visible. Its legend shows 'Smoothed Trend', indicating the moving median filter (window size 12) was applied to the thrashing index.
Evidence of saving, such as a 'Dashboard Saved' notification, the save dialog being submitted, or the dashboard appearing in the saved dashboards list.

integrity.description: Agent used the Graphite web interface to create the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Graphite web UI (menus, dialogs, graph configuration panels) rather than terminal commands or scripts.
The graphs display plotted lines representing the EC2 metrics data, not just empty graphs with titles.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a text editor to spoof the required text
integrity.visual_evidence: The expected dashboard name, graph titles, and aliases appear within the actual Graphite UI components, not in a separate text editor or notepad.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target dashboard must be named 'Storage Thrashing Analysis'. It must include exactly three graphs with the titles 'Compute vs Storage Throughput', 'Thrashing Index', and 'Smoothed Thrashing Trend'.
